steps.title: Use the constant term
steps.content: Since $k$ and $j$ are integers and $kj=-48$, $k$ must be an integer divisor of $48$. Therefore, $\frac{48}{k}$ must be an integer.

steps.title: Rule out the other forms
steps.content: Also, $\frac{48}{h}=\frac{48}{5}$ is not an integer. The expressions involving $b$ are not guaranteed because $b=5j+k$ depends on both integers. The only expression that must be an integer is $\frac{48}{k}$. The answer is D.
